error: op_kernel/average_pooling1d_custom_nvoyznvoipgmuqro20260310051705.cpp:70:110: error: cast between floating and unsigned integer variable is not allowed in aicore function
summary: AICore kernels should avoid runtime casts from uint32 attrs to float. For window-size divisors, compute a float reciprocal in host tiling and pass it to the kernel instead of dividing by static_cast<float>(kernelSize) inside the kernel.

PyTorch AvgPool1d defaults to count_include_pad=True. If the custom kernel divides by the number of valid input elements instead of kernel_size, border windows will fail correctness checks; use the fixed kernel-size divisor to match torch.nn.AvgPool1d semantics.

error: op_kernel/average_pooling1d_custom_jtdcogvawjhofoye20260310052252.cpp:68:84: error: cast between floating and unsigned integer variable is not allowed in aicore function
summary: When a compile error points to float/unsigned conversion around a reduction divisor, move the reciprocal computation to host tiling and store it as a float field. Kernel code should multiply by that float constant instead of converting uint32 attributes inside AICore code.

If an AvgPool-style kernel compiles and most values match but many positions still fail, the local-buffer DataCopy path may be corrupting or mis-handling the sliding-window computation. For small 1D pooling shapes, switching to direct GlobalTensor GetValue/SetValue access can recover stable correctness before reintroducing buffered optimization.
